jokes.text: All right, Lemon, you got me. Yes, this is the same flashy night tie I was wearing when I left work yesterday. My hair is a disaster. I am indeed on a walk of shame.
jokes.punchline: Formal corporate language for walk of shame admission
jokes.explanation: Strong character work - Jack's corporate formality applied to embarrassing situation creates good contrast

jokes.text: You slut!
jokes.setup: Jack confesses to walk of shame
jokes.punchline: Liz's mock-judgmental response
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - economy of language works well

jokes.text: That woman was Pizzarina Sbarro, the heiress to the Sbarro Slice and Calzone Fortune.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake Italian name based on Sbarro pizza chain
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist naming - 'Pizzarina Sbarro' is perfectly ridiculous and memorable

jokes.text: Come on. Walk of shame? I say call it a stride of pride and walk with your arms up, like this.
jokes.punchline: Liz's rebranding and presumably silly arm gesture
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with 'stride of pride' - gives episode its title

jokes.text: I stayed up all night helping Bradley finish the Joseph coat. Just hearing that story tells me that Bradley was gay. Only sexually.
jokes.setup: Liz mentions helping Bradley with Joseph coat
jokes.punchline: Bradley was gay 'only sexually' - absurd qualifier
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - 'only sexually' is a perfect absurd qualifier that subverts expectations

jokes.text: Liz Lemon has had a little awakening in her bathing suit area.
jokes.setup: Liz talking about her sexual awakening
jokes.punchline: Childish euphemism 'bathing suit area'
jokes.explanation: Perfect for Liz's character - awkward euphemism for adult topic

jokes.text: Lemon, I've seen your bathing suits. That could be anywhere.
jokes.punchline: Jack implies her bathing suits are shapeless/cover everything
jokes.explanation: Solid visual joke - implies Liz wears unflattering, coverage-heavy swimwear

jokes.text: Sex and the City Girls' brunches, where you talk about dirty stuff and make a lot of puns, like, 'and I thought I was eating eggs.'
jokes.punchline: Terrible example of sexual pun about eggs
jokes.explanation: Good satire of SATC dialogue - Liz's awkward attempt at innuendo

jokes.text: she's one of a diverse group of women I'm currently seeing. Group? Back to judgmental.
jokes.punchline: Liz immediately becomes judgmental again
jokes.explanation: Quick character beat - shows Liz's shifting moral stance

jokes.text: It's not fair to the ladies, just because I'm the, uh, complete package. You are a complete package.
jokes.setup: Jack claims he's too perfect for one woman
jokes.punchline: Liz's deadpan agreement
jokes.explanation: Liz enabling Jack's delusions - good character dynamic

jokes.text: I got the idea watching The Great Escape on TCM one night, how that ragtag bunch of heroes worked together to be greater than the sum of their parts. Bronson was the brawn, Attenborough was the brains, garner was the scrounger, McQueen was the hottie with the body. I'll say it.
jokes.punchline: Comparing women to POW movie characters, especially McQueen as 'hottie with the body'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd analogy - Jack's corporate mindset applied to dating via classic film

jokes.text: Who would like to join me for brunch? My treat. We could throw back some cosmos, talk about our climaxes...
jokes.punchline: Awkward sexual overshare to uncomfortable male colleagues
jokes.explanation: Good cringe - Liz misreading the room with sexual frankness

jokes.text: If you heard my sex stories, you would lose your mind!
jokes.punchline: Desperate overselling of her sex life
jokes.explanation: Character-consistent desperation - Liz trying too hard to seem worldly

jokes.text: Zarina is the society girl I take to black-tie events, when I want to talk politics I call Anne, Tabitha knows how to work my DVR, and Mindy is my, uh, sex idiot.
jokes.setup: Jack listing his different girlfriends' functions
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of 'sex idiot' as a category
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - mundane tasks to 'sex idiot' delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Sorry, Liz. I'm not even sure they serve brunch after... The '90s.
jokes.punchline: Brunch being dated to the '90s
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery - treating brunch as culturally extinct

jokes.text: Jenna Maroney, looking great at... 56? Blarf!
jokes.punchline: Horror at being called 56, plus 'Blarf!'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Jenna's vanity and 'Blarf!' exclamation

jokes.text: And if they did come to blows, it would be, uh... Intensely erotic. Like, uh, Steve McQueen on a motorcycle. Trying to jump that fence into Switzerland, but it's too high. He had a leather jacket.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre metaphor trailing off into random details about McQueen
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird - Jack's fantasy devolving into random movie details

jokes.text: God, no... I have children!
jokes.punchline: Non-sequitur about having children as if that's the real tragedy
jokes.explanation: Absurd non-sequitur - having children somehow worse than being 56

jokes.text: Pete, you go down to the newsstand. Buy every copy, and burn them. Cerie, get out of here. Just go home for the day.
jokes.setup: Crisis management for Jenna's age story
jokes.punchline: Telling young Cerie to leave because she'll make it worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - removing youth from premises to protect Jenna

jokes.text: I agree @therealstephenhawking. Women are not funny. Never have been. Never will be. Nerd rage!
jokes.punchline: Tracy's inflammatory tweet followed by 'Nerd rage!'
jokes.explanation: Sets up episode conflict - Tracy being provocatively wrong

jokes.text: Honestly, I never realized she was trying to be funny. I guess I just thought she was a wig model.
jokes.punchline: Tracy thought Jenna was just a wig model
jokes.explanation: Sharp insult - implies Jenna's hair is her only notable feature

jokes.text: You wouldn't ask an Asian person to give you a list of good Asian drivers. Gary Tang, Ziang Chu, Roy Chung... Stop it. I was done anyway.
jokes.setup: Liz refuses to list funny women
jokes.punchline: Tracy immediately lists Asian drivers, then claims he was finished
jokes.explanation: Great subversion - Tracy does exactly what Liz says not to do, then claims coincidental timing

jokes.text: Welcome to the flower shop...
jokes.punchline: Liz's childish voice attempt at humor
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad joke - shows Liz cracking under pressure with terrible attempt

jokes.text: Football. Motorcycles. Steak restaurants. Really dark superhero movies. These are things that suck.
jokes.punchline: Liz dismissing them all as things that suck
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of 'universal' appeal - Liz rejecting male-coded interests

jokes.text: See? That's the kind of hilarious button chicks can't come up with.
jokes.punchline: Tracy treating her exit as proof of his point
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke - Tracy using Liz's dramatic exit as evidence for his argument

jokes.text: Mmm, my lips are so dry. I better wet them with this mop.
jokes.setup: Kenneth doing rounds in Jenna's dressing room
jokes.punchline: Using a dirty mop to wet his lips
jokes.explanation: Gross visual gag - Kenneth's innocent obliviousness to hygiene

jokes.text: Oh, no. Now my undershirt is wet with mop juice.
jokes.punchline: Calling mop water 'mop juice'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - 'mop juice' is wonderfully gross and innocent

jokes.text: You be the janitor, and I'll be the piece of gum on the floor that you just can't chisel off. Sure is hot in here. Maybe I should roll up my pants. Oh, yeah. Show me that part of your shins that's hairless from your synthetic socks.
jokes.setup: Jenna realizes Kenneth is trying to seduce her
jokes.punchline: Jenna turning it into elaborate roleplay scenario with specific fetish details
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Jenna's sexual appetite turning Kenneth's innocence into elaborate fantasy

jokes.text: Uh, this is Mindy. Mindy, Zarina. Pleasure. Yeah. Oh, shiny.
jokes.punchline: Mindy's simple-minded 'Oh, shiny' reaction
jokes.explanation: Shows Mindy as the 'sex idiot' - distracted by shiny objects

jokes.text: And like a silver-backed gorilla or Mitt Romney's grandfather, I require more than one woman to...
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to gorilla and Romney's polygamist ancestor
jokes.explanation: Good absurd comparison - mixing animal behavior with political scandal

jokes.text: The woman appears to have no hip joints.
jokes.punchline: Clinical description of sexual flexibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack delivery - clinical assessment of sexual acrobatics

jokes.text: Another old guy wanted to buy my shirt. Old guys are so funny.
jokes.setup: Ryan arriving as Zarina's sex idiot
jokes.punchline: Calling old guys funny while talking to Jack
jokes.explanation: Unintentional insult to Jack by calling old guys 'funny'

jokes.text: Ryan is my sex idiot.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his sex idiot earlier
jokes.punchline: Zarina has her own sex idiot with same terminology
jokes.explanation: Good callback - reveals Jack isn't unique in his approach

jokes.text: I suggested that we wear sleep masks. I hit my nose pretty hard on the bedside table, but...
jokes.punchline: Sexual experiment gone wrong with furniture collision
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz - sexual adventure ending in physical injury

jokes.text: It's a monkey, and his name is Professor Wigglebottom.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous academic name for a monkey
jokes.explanation: Silly name works - contrast between formal title and ridiculous surname

jokes.text: So no women are funny, but you want me to put a monkey on the show.
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the logical inconsistency
jokes.explanation: Good logical point - highlights Tracy's absurd standards

jokes.text: Name one funny monkey. Bonzo, Clyde, the bear... No! I refuse to answer this question with a list.
jokes.punchline: Tracy lists monkeys while Liz uses her same response
jokes.explanation: Solid callback structure - reversing the earlier dynamic

jokes.text: My generation calls it pokemoning. Gotta catch 'em all.
jokes.punchline: Young person's Pokemon reference for casual dating
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture application - Pokemon collecting as dating metaphor

jokes.text: The plural of Pokemon is 'Pokemon.'
jokes.punchline: Correcting him on Pokemon grammar
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor - young person correcting old person on nerd culture

jokes.text: Mindy... Mindy... No, Ryan Lochte! Look at me... focus.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to get Mindy's attention
jokes.punchline: Having to use Olympic swimmer reference and jingling keys
jokes.explanation: Great visual - treating Mindy like a distracted child/animal

jokes.text: Teens gone wild: Is Jenna Maroney partying too hard? Be careful, you don't want to turn out like Amanda Byne-us. Did I do it right?
jokes.setup: Kenneth trying to make Jenna feel young
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing Bynes as 'Byne-us' and asking for approval
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's innocent attempt at modern reference with bad pronunciation

jokes.text: I watch Castle, and my purse is filled with sweet'n lows.
jokes.setup: Jenna claiming she's too old to party
jokes.punchline: Specific old-person behaviors as evidence
jokes.explanation: Good specific details - Castle and artificial sweeteners as age markers

jokes.text: If I find out that someone around here planted this disgusting story that I'm young, I'm gonna do to them what I did to my own ribs... Take them out.
jokes.setup: Jenna threatening whoever planted the young story
jokes.punchline: Threatening violence but revealing cosmetic surgery
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - sets up violence threat, delivers vanity confession

jokes.text: To escape the curse of the middle-aged actress. Instead of losing a push-up contest to Julie Bowen to see who gets to play Kevin James's mean wife who he's sick of having sex with, I'm gonna skip ahead to being an amazing slut who wins Oscars.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining her age manipulation strategy
jokes.punchline: Specific, degrading Hollywood scenario vs. 'amazing slut' ambition
jokes.explanation: Sharp Hollywood satire - specific degrading roles vs. aspirational 'amazing slut'

jokes.text: I mean, how hot is Helen Mirren? Super-hot. I mean, have you seen that picture of her in a bikini? She looks amazing for... Exactly... 'For.' She looks amazing for a 67-year-old. She's actually not that hot. She's got a gut and British legs.
jokes.setup: Jenna using Helen Mirren as example of older actress appeal
jokes.punchline: Deconstructing the 'for her age' qualifier into harsh reality
jokes.explanation: Smart deconstruction - how 'amazing for her age' actually works as backhanded compliment

jokes.text: I'm scared, but it tickles.
jokes.punchline: Confused reaction mixing fear and pleasure
jokes.explanation: Ambiguous reaction that could apply to many situations - good economy

jokes.text: I know Geri-Chair. They're the company that makes my bed steps.
jokes.punchline: Liz casually revealing she uses their products
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - Liz being old before her time

jokes.text: When Jack Welch called you Rick. And I was fine with that too. I mean, he and I had met only, like, a million times, so why should he remember me.
jokes.setup: Jack claiming he's fine with Zarina dating others
jokes.punchline: Revealing deep hurt about Jack Welch not knowing his name
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - Jack's corporate insecurity bleeding through

jokes.text: There are no bad ideas, Lemon, only great ideas that go horribly wrong.
jokes.punchline: Jack's twisted positive thinking philosophy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack philosophy - corporate optimism twisted into pessimism

jokes.text: I once saw a monkey in a cowboy outfit. I would love to see the town he's the sheriff of.
jokes.punchline: Imagining monkey cowboy as actual sheriff
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation - taking monkey costume to logical conclusion

jokes.text: We did it in the shower. My shoes got ruined. I said no!
jokes.setup: Jack refusing to hear sex details
jokes.punchline: Liz oversharing despite his protests
jokes.explanation: Good cringe - Liz's oversharing continues despite clear boundaries

jokes.text: See how he's wearing clothes? And he's got a suitcase like he's going on a business trip. Why's he being so professional?
jokes.setup: Showing Professor Wigglebottom to the writers
jokes.punchline: Analyzing monkey's professional demeanor
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist observation about monkey's business attire

jokes.text: He could play a young Steven Tyler. He looks like me if I were fancy.
jokes.punchline: Frank comparing himself unfavorably to a monkey
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecation - Frank's low self-esteem humor

jokes.text: Male nipples, funny. Monkey nipples, funny. Female nipples, useless!
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous categorization of nipples by humor value
jokes.explanation: Absurd categorization typical of Tracy's bizarre logic

jokes.text: Why didn't she write, 'thinking of you, Jackie-bear?' She could have written this... to anyone.
jokes.punchline: Overanalyzing generic endearment
jokes.explanation: Good character neurosis - Jack wanting personalized pet names

jokes.text: Jenna and I are re-mounting our award-winning 1996 two-woman show.
jokes.punchline: Claiming their old show was 'award-winning'
jokes.explanation: Sets up upcoming performance - 'award-winning' likely exaggerated

jokes.text: This woman is pokemoning me. She's blind-copied you, but if you just click this plus sign...
jokes.punchline: Old person needing tech help to understand modern dating
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor - tech illiteracy meets dating insecurity

jokes.text: kentremendous@fremulon.biz... Ugh. totalpackage58... Wait, that's me.
jokes.punchline: His own ridiculous email address
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack characterization - 'totalpackage58' is hilariously on-brand

jokes.text: Well, that was Gerald Chair, the inventor of the Geri-Chair. They're leaning towards Jamie Lee Curtis. She already beat me out for Activia, and cold flash menopause popsicles.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous elderly-targeted products
jokes.explanation: 'Cold flash menopause popsicles' is wonderfully absurd product concept

jokes.text: Today's the day when I'm gonna do my first favor ever.
jokes.punchline: Jenna admitting this would be her first favor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna - extreme narcissism revealed casually

jokes.text: Are you thinking, or doing kegels?
jokes.punchline: Liz can't tell if it's thinking or pelvic exercises
jokes.explanation: Good visual joke - similar facial expressions for different activities

jokes.text: I look forward to discovering exactly what each of you has to offer Zarina that I do not.
jokes.punchline: Corporate approach to romantic jealousy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack - treating romance like a business analysis

jokes.text: No, I'm pretty sure we're in love.
jokes.setup: Jack categorizing him as sex idiot
jokes.punchline: Ryan thinking it's a real relationship
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Jack's cynicism and Ryan's naivety

jokes.text: Heavenly father. You must be Ken tremendous.
jokes.punchline: Jack's religious exclamation at seeing him
jokes.explanation: Sets up Ken's impressive appearance

jokes.text: We've covered all the classic boyfriend archetypes. Except the father figure. Where is that guy, am I right? The one who falls asleep at the opera, and doesn't notice that she's texting her real boyfriend from his bed. Where's that sucker?
jokes.setup: Jack categorizing all the boyfriend types
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's describing himself as the father figure
jokes.explanation: Great building realization - Jack unknowingly describing his own role

jokes.text: Oh, no... Is it me?
jokes.punchline: Dawning horror at self-recognition
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff - Jack's ego crashing into reality

jokes.text: Doctor, I'm unhappy with me widdle body. Can you help me?
jokes.punchline: Baby talk about plastic surgery
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - child wanting cosmetic surgery in baby voice

jokes.text: Well, of course. Toddler plastic surgery is an exciting new field. We can take you down an entire diaper size.
jokes.punchline: Doctor enthusiastically offering toddler procedures
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - 'diaper size' is perfectly absurd medical term

jokes.text: 'Tank' you, doctor. Don't thank me, thank Roe V. Wade.
jokes.punchline: Crediting abortion rights for cosmetic surgery access
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark turn - connecting reproductive rights to cosmetic surgery

jokes.text: That was actually funny, Liz. So why do TGS suck so much?
jokes.punchline: Immediately insulting their regular work
jokes.explanation: Good backhanded compliment - praise immediately undercut

jokes.text: When you were, like, 'come in, I'm a doctor,' and you had on that lab coat, like a doctor. A lady doctor... Oh my God, that is hilarious!
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining what he found funny
jokes.punchline: Thinking the joke was just a female doctor existing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy misunderstanding - missing the actual joke completely

jokes.text: And Jenna, you as that sad old prostitute trying to look young... Commentary!
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstanding her character as prostitute
jokes.explanation: Tracy's continued misunderstanding - seeing prostitute instead of child

jokes.text: There's nothing older than trying to be young. And you looked like a million years up there. Congratulations. You are Geri-Chair's new spokes-crone!
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment leading to 'spokes-crone' title
jokes.explanation: 'Spokes-crone' is excellent portmanteau - perfect for Jenna's situation

jokes.text: Sometimes, I like to come up here at night and flash my breasts at the empire state building.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre exhibitionist ritual with landmark
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna - narcissistic exhibitionism with NYC landmark

jokes.text: Is that the only building that you flash? Or do you also flash the Time Warner center to make the Empire State Building feel like an old fool.
jokes.punchline: Jack imagining jealousy between buildings
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - anthropomorphizing buildings with Jack's relationship anxiety

jokes.text: I mean, inside, I still feel like a sex idiot. But I have to accept the fact that I'm fake 56 now, and I'm more of a Diane Lane ageless beauty.
jokes.punchline: Casually referencing her fake age and aspirational beauty category
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Jenna's delusional self-image

jokes.text: Do you really want this girl asking you to go hear her friend DJ in Brooklyn? No, that sounds exhausting. Do you want to drive five hours to go rock climbing with her, and be expected to have sex after? I do not. I mean, my back.
jokes.setup: Jenna helping Jack embrace being older
jokes.punchline: Specific young person activities being exhausting
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor - specific activities that sound awful to older people

jokes.text: You get to say racist stuff whenever you want, and people bring you soup.
jokes.setup: Jenna listing benefits of being older
jokes.punchline: Casual racism and soup as elderly privileges
jokes.explanation: Dark observation about how society excuses elderly behavior

jokes.text: I do like soup.
jokes.punchline: Jack focusing on the innocent part
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Jack missing the problematic part

jokes.text: Or can we agree that we're all just monkeys with suitcases trying to seem like people?
jokes.punchline: Referencing Professor Wigglebottom as metaphor for humanity
jokes.explanation: Nice philosophical callback - using monkey metaphor for human condition

jokes.text: Wait... You're a female? How you doin', girl?
jokes.punchline: Immediately hitting on the monkey
jokes.explanation: Tracy being Tracy - inappropriate response to discovering monkey's gender

jokes.text: Phyllis Diller, Joan Rivers, Gilda Radner, Lucille ball, Julia Louis-Dreyfus, Carol Burnett, Lily Tomlin. Yes, thank you, Tracy. Those are all very funny women. Funny women? Those are the names of my fingers.
jokes.setup: Liz thinking Tracy is listing funny women
jokes.punchline: Tracy revealing they're his finger names
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - subverts expectation that Tracy learned lesson

jokes.text: Irma Bombeck, Tig Notaro... Is my impression of a Chinese person.
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's his racist impression
jokes.explanation: Pushes boundaries but feels more crude than clever

jokes.text: Ellen DeGeneres, Mo'nique, and Roseanne Barr. Now that's some funny women. All of whom have screamed at me because they were on their period.
jokes.setup: Tracy seemingly listing funny women correctly
jokes.punchline: Undermining it with menstruation comment
jokes.explanation: Tracy staying in character - can't give women credit without backhanded comment
